summary: Regression testing completed after 3 changes: DOMPurify sanitization, Cloudinary integration, and Dockerfiles. All existing features working correctly. No regressions found.
changes_tested: DOMPurify sanitization in ArticlePage.js - HTML content preserved correctly with ADD_TAGS for iframe/blockquote
Cloudinary upload endpoint - Returns 500 as expected when not configured, server doesn't crash
Dockerfiles added - Not tested in preview environment (deployment-only)

minor.issue: Returns 500 when Cloudinary not configured - EXPECTED behavior
minor.impact: No impact - this is expected in dev environment without Cloudinary keys
No functional impact - fallback ticker data works correctly

console_warnings.warning: Cannot update a component while rendering a different component (BrowserRouter/AdminLogin)
console_warnings.impact: No functional impact - login works correctly

passed_tests: Article page at /crypto/bitcoin-surges-past-100k-institutional-demand renders with DOMPurify sanitized content

HTML content preserved after sanitization (

,

tags present)
Homepage loads with all sections (Latest News, Crypto, Press Releases, Sponsored)
Homepage sections API: GET /api/articles/homepage-sections returns all sections
Admin login at /admin/login with petar010591@gmail.com / Zvezda2023! - SUCCESS
Admin dashboard loads with stats (10 articles, 10 published, 7 categories, 1 team)
XML sitemap at /api/sitemap.xml returns valid XML with all URLs
Author profile page at /author/69cd3fb3b6ffab906a8470e1 shows author info and 10 articles
Navigation from homepage article card to article page works
OG meta tags present on article page (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url)
Upload endpoint POST /api/upload returns 500 when Cloudinary not configured (expected)
Server continues running after upload failure (no crash)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 changes verified: 1) DOMPurify sanitization working - article HTML content renders correctly with preserved tags. 2) Cloudinary upload returns 500 as expected when not configured - server handles gracefully. 3) Dockerfiles not tested (deployment-only). All 25 backend tests pass. Frontend fully functional including admin dashboard, article pages, author profiles, and navigation.
